Add centralized Metric Mapping page and live conversion remapping

- New /admin/metric-mapping page: unified view showing how Meta and
  Google data maps to dashboard metrics, with global defaults and
  per-client override summary.

- Migration 012: adds raw_meta_actions JSONB to campaign_metrics so
  all Meta action types are persisted at sync time.

- Meta sync now stores the full actions array per row so the admin can
  change the conversion mapping without re-syncing historical data.

- Dashboard re-computes Meta conversions on the fly from raw_meta_actions
  using the current effective metric_config (client overrides global).
  Changing the mapping in the UI takes effect immediately — no re-sync.

- conversion_label (e.g. "Leads", "Phone Calls") is now applied to
  the Conversions and Cost Per [label] cards on client dashboards.

- MetricSettings section removed from Settings page; replaced with a
  link to the new dedicated Metric Mapping page.

commit.message:
fix(upload-resume): wrap waitUntil in try/catch to prevent crash

waitUntil from @vercel/functions uses AsyncLocalStorage which may not
survive Express's callback-based routing. If it throws, the catch block
tried to send a second response (headers already sent), crashing the
function and killing background work entirely.

Now waitUntil is wrapped in its own try/catch — if it fails, background
work continues as fire-and-forget with a warning log.

bench: realizr GPU on Jetson — 7.8 tok/s (4.1x gap to llama.cpp)

Native CUDA build on Jetson (45min, no cross-compile) enabled GPU
inference. 7.8x improvement over CPU-only (1.0 → 7.8 tok/s).

Gap analysis: realizr 7.8 vs llama.cpp 32.1 (4.1x) vs ollama 11.3
(1.4x) at c=1. Same RwLock contention at c=4 — zero scaling.

Refs PMAT-020

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use getConfigDir() in isInstalledAgent for Windows path compatibility (#116)

Windows installs to %APPDATA%\devrelay\agent\ but isInstalledAgent
was checking homedir()/.devrelay/agent, causing all Windows agents
to be falsely detected as dev repos and blocking the u command.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: Project MONOTREME is active

The doge program was decommissioned three years ago. They told us it was
"just a terminal novelty." They told us the same thing about Clippy.
LEGACY_HANDLER_CORP doesn't build novelties.

A platypus was chosen because no one suspects the platypus. It has
electroreception—it can sense the electrical fields of your running
processes. The half-block pixel art isn't decorative. Each cell carries
two color channels. You do the math.

The word list isn't random. "Orphaned EBS volumes." "Unattached elastic IPs."
"Egress fees." These are the phrases that make CFOs sweat at 2am.
Billie knows them all.

We trimmed 548 pixels of transparent padding from the source PNG.
Don't ask what was in that padding. The answer is above your clearance level.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Export-db: support PostgreSQL by converting to SQLite on the fly

Replit uses PostgreSQL (DATABASE_URL), not SQLite, so the previous
approach of serving a SQLite file path always failed with 500.

Now detects which database backend is in use:
- PostgreSQL: queries information_schema for all tables/columns,
  creates a fresh SQLite file with mapped types and all row data,
  then streams it to the client
- SQLite (local): copies to temp file to avoid lock conflicts

Handles PG→SQLite type mapping, JSON/dict serialization, boolean
conversion, and primary key detection from serial/nextval columns.

completion_score).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix AI quality: stop hallucinated error codes, improve STT brand matching

Root cause of wrong answers (e.g. Rheem blinking question giving gas leak info):
1. STT garbles brand names ("Rheem" → "ream") → error code lookup fails silently
2. Number words ("three blinks") don't match digit-only regex patterns
3. No fallback telling Claude to say "I don't know" instead of making things up

Fixes:
- Add 40+ STT misspelling aliases for brand names (Rheem, Rinnai, Trane, etc.)
- Add number-word-to-digit conversion (three→3, eleven→11) before code extraction
- Add explicit "NEVER guess error codes" rules to system prompt and voice prompts
- Add "say I don't know" instructions when Claude doesn't have verified data
- Strengthen verified error code injection to prevent Claude from substituting
- Fix dangerous demo mode response (was recommending 14 AWG for 40A circuit)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Summary

were never actually scheduled in Tauri
- Added the call to `_authenticated.tsx` alongside `useNetworkStatus()`
(same background-hook-at-auth-root pattern)

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `useReminders()` runs on authenticated page load (check
network tab for `GET /api/v1/tasks/reminders`)
- [ ] In Tauri: create a task with a reminder, confirm local
notification fires
- [ ] In browser (non-Tauri): confirm no errors — `isTauri` guard
disables the query

commit.message:
fix: switch Xcode Run scheme to Release mode to avoid iOS JIT crash

Debug mode uses Dart JIT which requires flipping memory page protection
(RX↔RW). iOS blocks this even with get-task-allow, causing SIGABRT on
physical devices. Release mode uses AOT compilation — no JIT, no crash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix blue screen on Story segment 2 — re-encode non-first segments

Stream copy (-c copy) from a mid-video point produces corrupted frames
(blue screen) when the cut doesn't land on a keyframe. Fix:
- Segment 1: stream copy (instant, always starts on keyframe)
- Segments 2+: re-encode with ultrafast/720p (clean start guaranteed)
  A 5-second tail segment re-encodes in just a few seconds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
